info.title: About
info.intro: A short overview of Sparkki and an optional in-browser Linux trial before you decide.
info.tryLinuxIntro: Choose Linux Mint or Fedora. Opens in a new tab via noVNC to the lab server. This is a demo: do not enter sensitive data.
info.mintBody: Familiar Cinnamon workflow and a calm upgrade cadence — a gentle first step.
info.fedoraBody: Newer toolchain and strong developer defaults — try this if you want a fresher stack.
info.tryLinuxSecurityHint: Security: this is a lab / demo environment. Do not enter passwords or personal data. For production use HTTPS (e.g. infra/try-linux docker-compose.tls.yml with Caddy), set a shared access_token (TRY_LINUX_ACCESS_TOKEN / NEXT_PUBLIC_TRY_LINUX_ACCESS_TOKEN), and firewall appropriately. Raw VNC ports (59xx) must not be exposed to the internet — only behind the websockify proxy.
info.notConfigured: Browser demo is not wired yet. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_TRY_LINUX_PROXY_BASE (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8080) and deploy infra/try-linux.

hub.metaTitle: Learn — give your PC a second life
hub.metaDescription: Why upgrading older hardware with Linux is practical, what software and games are compatible, app lists, the upgrade process, and recommended distributions.
hub.title: Your old computer can still do a lot
hub.intro: An SSD, optional RAM, and a light Linux install can turn a sluggish laptop or desktop into a fast daily driver — without buying a whole new machine.
hub.introSub: Most everyday apps have solid Linux versions or open alternatives. Gaming coverage keeps growing thanks to Steam and Proton, and work-from-home stacks are largely there — not perfect for every niche game or vendor-locked app, but broader than many expect.
hub.benefitsTitle: Why this upgrade path is viable
hub.benefitsLead: The win is both hardware (storage, memory) and software (less background churn). That combo is what people feel Monday morning when the machine just opens and stays responsive.
hub.b1Body: SSD boot and app launches are dramatically shorter; Linux keeps the OS lighter so the hardware is not fighting constant maintenance tasks.
hub.b2Body: Extending life cuts e-waste and postpones a purchase when the chassis, keyboard, and screen are still perfectly usable.
hub.b3Body: Updates are predictable; the consumer annoyance profile is usually quieter than many older Windows installs stuck on spinning disks.
hub.b4Body: Browsers, office work, calls, creative tools, and a large slice of Steam libraries — always check edge cases, but the overlap is large and growing.
hub.compatBody: Linux is not a blank slate: Steam, Proton, and native titles cover much of casual and mainstream gaming. Productivity stacks — browsers, chat, LibreOffice, developer editors, creative apps — are maintained and installable.
hub.compatLi1: Many Steam games run natively or via Proton; ProtonDB is the sanity check for specific titles.
hub.compatLi2: Web apps behave like on any OS — docs, school portals, banking in the browser.
hub.compatLi3: Peripherals and video calls usually work on common hardware without hand-tuning.
hub.compatLi4: Specialist or vendor-locked software may need a plan B — we tell you plainly when something is a poor fit.
hub.appsSectionIntro: Concrete swaps for programs you already use on Windows or Mac — from browsers to creative suites.
hub.appsWinBody: Common programs with mature Linux installers or equivalents.
hub.osSectionIntro: We focus on friendly, well-supported desktops. For most upgraders we recommend Linux Mint for familiarity and Fedora Workstation for a polished, up-to-date GNOME experience.
hub.osMintBody: Cinnamon feels familiar if you know classic Windows, the Software Manager keeps installs simple, and the release pace favors stability — our default for many installs.
hub.osFedoraBody: Polished GNOME desktop, strong defaults upstream, excellent for developers or households that want newer packages with solid QA.
hub.osFedoraNote: We can install either depending on your habits — ask if you are unsure which fits best.

demo.sectionIntro: Stylized previews of everyday workloads — not your exact machine, but the feel of productivity, play, creativity, and the web.
demo.productivityCaption: Files, browser tabs, and chat side by side without the crawl of an HDD system drive.
demo.gamingCaption: A growing share of titles run natively or via Proton — check specific games before you bet a purchase on one title.
demo.dailyCaption: School portals, streaming, and banking behave like you expect in a modern browser.
demo.creativeCaption: Edits, exports, and hobby creative setups that do not require a brand-new workstation.

galleria.metaDescription: Large slideshow views of productivity, gaming, creativity, and the web on a Linux desktop.
galleria.title: Demo gallery
galleria.intro: Stylized desktop scenes — not screenshots from your exact PC, but a feel for how multitasking, games, creative work, and browsing can look on a light Linux setup.

hyodyt.metaDescription: Older hardware that still feels fast, a license-free OS stack, security and privacy — plus Sparkki’s Finnish service.
hyodyt.title: Why this path works for you
hyodyt.intro: A quick overview of what you gain when you refresh an older computer with Linux through Sparkki — beyond raw speed.
hyodyt.highlightsIntro: Three themes we hear about a lot — ageing hardware, how pricing works, and who really controls the machine.
hyodyt.hwHighlightTitle: Older hardware can still feel quick and light
hyodyt.hwHighlightLead: A lightweight OS plus the right disk (SSD) changes how long hardware stays pleasant — often more than bolting the latest consumer Windows onto the same box.
hyodyt.hwHighlightP1: Boot and app opens feel dramatically better than a typical old HDD + Windows setup.
hyodyt.hwHighlightP2: Fewer background services and ads fighting for CPU — capacity stays for your tasks.
hyodyt.hwHighlightP3: Many laptops and desktops from the last 5–10 years are still perfectly viable for browsing, office work, school, and video calls.
hyodyt.freeHighlightTitle: The OS and open apps — no yearly license fee
hyodyt.freeHighlightLead: Desktop Linux and much of the software around it are free to use: you pay Sparkki for service (and any parts in your order), not an operating-system subscription.
hyodyt.freeHighlightP1: No mandatory Microsoft or Apple account just to keep the OS usable in this stack.
hyodyt.freeHighlightP2: Transparent source-led ecosystem with community and vendor maintenance — security fixes are visible and documented.
hyodyt.freeHighlightP3: You can largely rebuild the same stack on the next machine without a fresh “OS tax”.
hyodyt.secHighlightLead: The computer is your tool — not a channel for daily platform upsell baked into the install.
hyodyt.secHighlightP1: Updates are deliberate: maintainers patch vulnerabilities without a retail calendar forcing new paid tiers for basics.
hyodyt.secHighlightP2: Default telemetry and ad profiling are usually lighter than on many consumer Windows installs.
hyodyt.secHighlightP3: Files and accounts stay on your disk — cloud and sync are opt-in when you choose them.
hyodyt.b1Body: Core desktop work runs on your PC. You are not tied to a cloud login for the operating system, and everyday tasks do not depend on always-on online services unless you choose them.
hyodyt.b2Body: We are a Finnish operation: clear communication in Finnish (and English when you need it), local context, and terms that match how people here expect to be served.
hyodyt.b3Body: Reviving hardware you already own — especially with an SSD — is usually much cheaper than buying a new computer for the same browsing, office, and school tasks.
hyodyt.b4Body: Thoughtful updates and a lightweight stack mean fewer dramatic surprises than many ageing Windows installs: the machine stays predictable for daily work.
hyodyt.b5Body: Linux is a serious gaming platform today: Steam, native titles, and Proton cover a large part of popular libraries. Always check a specific game, but the overlap is much wider than a few years ago.
hyodyt.b6Body: Documents and files live on your disk by default. You decide what gets synced or uploaded — no platform quietly treating your stuff as someone else’s product.

linux.metaDescription: What happens once your machine reaches us: a fresh Linux install, SSD and RAM upgrades, recommended distributions — and what to do about your old HDD before shipping.
linux.title: Upgrade process
linux.intro: After your computer arrives with us, we rebuild it from a clean slate: we install a new operating system onto the SSD you selected and apply the hardware upgrades in your order (for example extra RAM). Below is a plain-language checklist of what we recommend and how to handle an old hard drive.
linux.processTitle: What we do once the device is here
linux.processStep1: We install a fresh Linux system on the new boot drive — not an in-place upgrade on top of Windows, but a clean install on the chosen disk.
linux.processStep2: We install the hardware agreed in your tier: typically a new SSD as the system drive and, when the board and slots allow it, a RAM upgrade.
linux.processStep3: We finish onboarding: your user account, networking, and any optional software bundles from the order.
linux.osIntro: We recommend two well-supported desktop distributions; the choice depends on how you use the machine, and we can install either.
linux.osMint: Linux Mint (Cinnamon) — our primary pick when you want a familiar desktop layout and a conservative update cadence.
linux.osFedora: Fedora Workstation — a strong alternative when you want a polished, up-to-date GNOME experience with solid upstream QA.
linux.hardwareSsd: An SSD as the system drive — the largest day-to-day win versus an old spinning disk for boot, apps, and responsiveness.
linux.hardwareRam: More RAM when the machine supports it — smoother multitasking, browsers, and light creative workloads.
linux.hddBody: If you want to keep the old HDD, we can leave it inside the machine as a second disk. We can mount it so your previous files stay available in the file manager, and add desktop shortcuts if you like — mention this in your order. We still recommend removing the HDD before you send the laptop: lower shipping weight and heat, fewer dual-drive laptop quirks, and simpler logistics. You can remove it yourself (see DIY guides) or choose paid removal in the order.
linux.tryHint: Want a feel for the desktop before you decide? Try the safe in-browser demo below.

vakaus.metaDescription: What stability means day to day on Linux and why it matters.
vakaus.title: Stability and comfort
vakaus.intro: A well-set-up Linux install is a quiet partner: boots quickly, does not nag mid-work, and stays readable for years.
vakaus.p1: Linux Mint’s conservative cadence means mature updates without constant experimentation. Displays and peripherals often work out of the box.
vakaus.p2: A lightweight desktop plus an SSD is the tangible upgrade people feel on older hardware — less waiting, less background noise.
vakaus.p3: If a device is borderline, we tell you before money changes hands.

huolia.supportTitle: You are not left alone with the machine
huolia.supportBody: The biggest worry is often not Linux itself. It is the idea of getting stuck later without help. Sparkki is structured to avoid that.
huolia.supportPoint1: We explain the handoff in plain language instead of assuming prior knowledge.
huolia.supportPoint2: Included support covers the first steps after delivery.
huolia.supportPoint3: If a device or workflow is a bad fit, we say so directly before work starts.
huolia.supportPoint4: Sparkki Care remains available if you want a longer support path.
huolia.metaDescription: Straight answers to frequent questions before moving to Linux.
huolia.title: Common concerns
huolia.intro: Short answers to questions we hear often.

q1.question: Will I lose my files?
q1.answer: Before install we back up or transfer important data when agreed. We always confirm before touching disks.

q2.question: Can I actually use Linux?
q2.answer: Most tasks feel the same: browser, email, files. We guide onboarding and support continues afterwards.

q3.question: What about Word and Excel?
q3.answer: LibreOffice opens and saves common document formats. Complex templates may need a quick check — we are honest about fit.

q4.question: Is it safe?
q4.answer: Linux updates in a controlled way and the threat profile differs from Windows. Basics still matter: backups and strong passwords.

q5.question: Will my camera / printer work?
q5.answer: Most USB peripherals work immediately. Exceptions we catch before the order or on intake.

q6.question: What if something goes wrong?
q6.answer: Included email support and the community help. Sparkki Care adds ongoing support when you want it later.

apps.introWin: Typical Windows programs and open alternatives for Linux.
apps.introMac: Typical macOS programs and alternatives for Linux.

care.tiersIntro: Choose a light monthly support layer if you want a calmer path after delivery instead of starting from zero each time.
care.benefitsIntro: Care exists for people who want continued clarity, not just a repaired machine.
care.benefitEmailBody: You can ask without rebuilding the full context every time.
care.benefitPriorityTitle: Faster path when something feels off
care.benefitPriorityBody: Priority handling means less waiting when you need reassurance or a quick fix.
care.benefitHealthBody: The higher tiers add proactive check-ins so the device stays pleasant to use.
care.timelineIntro: The transition from included support to Care is staged clearly so the end of the free period does not come as a surprise.
care.ctaPoint1: A known contact path beats searching for help from scratch.
care.ctaPoint2: You keep the machine understandable even months after delivery.
care.ctaPoint3: Support stays available in Finnish or English depending on your order.
care.ctaPoint4: You can also ask about upgrades later if your needs change.
care.metaDescription: After the included 90-day email support: remote help, priority channel, and check-ins.
care.title: Sparkki Care
care.intro: When the included 90 days end, Care keeps you comfortable with Linux for the long term.
care.tierPlusDesc: Everything in Basic + priority Discord and short remote sessions.
care.tierProDesc: Everything in Care+ + annual 30 min health call and upgrade summary.
care.tierHigherContact: Care+ and Care Pro are tailored — ask us for the right tier and pricing.
care.subscribeBlurb: Start a Basic monthly subscription. You will get a confirmation email after payment.
care.subscribeOrderHint: Helps us link support to a previous Sparkki service order.
care.subscribeErrorRateLimit: Too many attempts. Wait a moment and try again.

care.subscribeErrorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
care.thankYouBody: Your payment was confirmed. You will receive an email shortly. You can manage your subscription in the Stripe customer portal, or ask support for help.
care.ctaNote: Care+ and Care Pro — contact us to agree on scope (priority, remote sessions, or annual call).

forGood.bannerLead: Reduced SSD service pricing for approved applicants.

about.title: About us
about.intro: Sparkki is a small Finnish company. We refurbish laptops and desktops with SSD upgrades, extra RAM, and Linux — for homes and businesses.
about.companyNote: VAT liable. We operate in Finland.
about.person1Bio: Managing person at the company. Focus on service delivery, technical direction, and customer projects.
about.person2Bio: Managing person at the company. Focus on business development, partnerships, and customer relationships.

tilaus.introHub: Enter your order ID (from the confirmation email) and the same email you used when ordering.
tilaus.introPrefill: Enter the same email you used when ordering. The order ID is prefilled.
tilaus.privacyHint: Details are never shown without a matching email. Do not share your order link with strangers.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.metaDescription: How Sparkki handles personal data on the website and in orders.
privacy.intro: This is a short customer-facing summary. Replace with your legal register description where required.
privacy.section1Body: Sparkki Oy. Contact details: see About us.
privacy.section2Body: Order forms: name, email, phone, and address for fulfilment. Payments are processed by Stripe; we do not store card numbers on our servers.
privacy.section3Body: We may use Plausible (cookieless). Configure via NEXT_PUBLIC_PLAUSIBLE_DOMAIN.
privacy.section4Body: Order records are kept for accounting and support as required by law. You may request correction or erasure via our support where applicable.
privacy.section5Body: We update this page when our practices change.

home.title: Your computer deserves a second life
home.subtitle: We install an SSD, more RAM, and Linux. Fast boot, safer use, smaller footprint.
home.speedVerdict: Typical win: much shorter boot time with an SSD and a lighter system.
home.step1: Tell us your model or share specs
home.pricingFootnote: A spinning hard drive (HDD) slows boot — an SSD and a light Linux install are where most users feel the win. Exact packages and optional data transfer: Service page.
home.pricingForGoodLink: In a support group? Apply for a discounted price (Sparkki for Good).

palvelu.title: Service
palvelu.intro: We refurbish laptops and desktops. Tell us about your machine — we handle the rest.
palvelu.howIntro: The service flow should feel simple from the first compatibility check to the return of the finished machine.
palvelu.how1: Share brand and model or send specs.
palvelu.how2: See compatibility and estimated speed benefit.
palvelu.how4: Choose support level and enter contact details.
palvelu.how5: Pay securely with Stripe — confirmation by email.
palvelu.flow1Body: Send the model, age, or specs. We verify whether the machine is still worth refreshing before you commit.
palvelu.flow2Body: We recommend the right mix of SSD, memory, setup, and optional data migration based on your real use.
palvelu.flow3Body: Choose the easiest route: local pickup, bring it yourself, or ship it from elsewhere in Finland.
palvelu.flow4Title: Get it back ready to use
palvelu.flow4Body: We return the machine configured, tested, and explained in plain language so daily use feels calm again.
palvelu.pricingNote: Prices include VAT. Exact total is shown at checkout.
palvelu.storyIntro: A Sparkki refresh should feel like a practical improvement, not a technical hobby project.
palvelu.storyBefore3: Uncertainty about whether the machine is still worth anything
palvelu.storyAfter2: Clear setup for everyday work, school, and home tasks
palvelu.storyAfter3: A machine that feels stable and understandable again
palvelu.logisticsIntro: The hardware work matters, but so does the handoff. We explain the practical parts clearly before anything moves.
palvelu.logisticsPickupBody: Pickup, drop-off, or shipping depending on where you are and what is easiest for you.
palvelu.logisticsPackagingBody: We tell you how to pack the device safely so the trip itself does not become the risky part.
palvelu.logisticsTurnaroundBody: You get a realistic time window instead of vague promises, including what may affect the schedule.
palvelu.logisticsHandoffBody: When the machine comes back, the setup and the next steps are explained in normal language.
palvelu.migrationFaqTitle: Data transfer from Windows to Linux
palvelu.migrationFaqIntro: You can add an optional data migration when you order the service.
palvelu.migrationFaqA1: Depending on your order, we can move files, photos, bookmarks, and email account settings. Games and Windows-only apps may not run on Linux — mention your needs in the order notes.
palvelu.migrationFaqA2: The add-on (+€35 or +€50) appears in your order summary and on the Stripe checkout page. It is optional.
palvelu.migrationFaqA3: Yes, if the machine supports dual boot. Mention this in notes or when you order.
palvelu.backupsP1: When we set up backup or system snapshot tooling for you (for example scheduled snapshots to an external drive), it runs in the background on a schedule. Day-to-day use is not interrupted by constant pop-ups once the plan is in place.
palvelu.backupsP2: With handoff you receive written step-by-step instructions for restoring files or rolling back a snapshot, in Finnish or English to match your order — including what to do if the system will not boot normally (for example using a live USB).
palvelu.backupsPoint1: Written restore instructions are included with handoff.
palvelu.backupsPoint2: Language and examples match your order language.
palvelu.backupsPoint3: We explain the plan for both ordinary restores and boot problems.
palvelu.supportIntro: The point is not just to install software. It is to make the whole change understandable and calm for a normal person.
palvelu.supportHumanBody: Questions are answered directly and in plain language, before and after the service.
palvelu.supportTransparentBody: We tell you what is included, what is optional, and when something is not worth doing.
palvelu.supportGuidesBody: Written notes and setup guidance reduce the fear of "what if I forget something later?"
palvelu.supportCareBody: If you want support after the included period, Sparkki Care continues the relationship without making it feel like a helpdesk maze.
palvelu.b2bBanner: Need several machines or a business quote?

components.title: Parts — what we install
components.intro: We use new brand-name SSDs and memory modules. Below are typical models and specs; the final choice depends on what your machine and motherboard support. The link opens a Verkkokauppa.com search for a matching product (retail price may vary).
components.reassurance: Components are new and covered by the manufacturer’s standard consumer warranty. If your device needs a less common module, we tell you before work begins.

ssdSata.lead: Typical upgrade when the machine has a 2.5" SATA bay.

ssdNvme.lead: Faster storage when the board supports M.2 NVMe.

ram8gb.lead: DDR4 module, SODIMM or DIMM depending on the chassis — we pick a compatible kit.

palvelu.wizardLegalHint: By continuing you accept the order terms and processing of personal data to fulfill the order.

wizard.title: Order
wizard.stepHint0: Describe your machine — model or a short free-text description is enough.
wizard.stepHint1: Pick a service package and how the device travels to us and back. App bundles and the portable VM are optional — open the add-ons section if you want them.
wizard.stepHint2: Choose whether the old HDD is removed and who does it. Removal with SSD install is recommended.
wizard.stepHint3: Enter phone or email, review the summary, then continue to secure Stripe checkout.
wizard.computerPlaceholder: e.g. Lenovo ThinkPad T450, an old 2016 HP laptop, or: I do not know the model
wizard.computerHint: An exact model number is not required — we verify before starting work.
wizard.step2AddonsHint: Optional — skip if you only need the base service.
wizard.deliveryHomeSub: We collect from your door — Helsinki area
wizard.deliveryPostSub: You ship to us, we ship back — all of Finland
wizard.deliverySelfSub: Bring to an agreed point — Helsinki
wizard.hddCalloutBody: We recommend removing the old HDD so the SSD has room and thermals stay healthy. You can DIY or choose Sparkki to remove it (no extra charge on the full-service package).
wizard.hddGuideLink: See the DIY guide under “Tee itse”
wizard.hddSparkkiPaid: Sparkki removes the HDD for me (+€20)
wizard.hddSparkkiIncluded: Sparkki removes the HDD for me (included in package)
wizard.hddSelf: I will remove the HDD myself (+€0)
wizard.hddSparkkiDesc: Recommended — we detach the drive during prep.
wizard.hddSelfDesc: Save money — follow the removal guide carefully.
wizard.hddKeep: I will keep the HDD in the machine (+€0)
wizard.hddKeepDesc: Not recommended with two drives in a laptop — ask us if unsure.
wizard.bundlesHint: Pick one or more — we install and configure them as part of the service.
wizard.vmTitle: Portable VM or disk image of your current system
wizard.vmHint: Optional add-on before we install Linux: we produce an agreed-format image of the machine state. Not a guarantee of every hidden OEM partition or firmware mode — exact scope is agreed at intake.
wizard.vmEnableAddon: Add portable VM / disk image to order
wizard.vmDisableAddon: Remove portable VM / disk image from order
wizard.vmHandoffCustomerTitle: Your USB drive or NAS at handoff
wizard.vmHandoffCustomerDesc: You provide enough empty or pre-formatted media when you drop off the machine or at the agreed pickup point.
wizard.vmHandoffShippedDesc: We arrange separately if you need a mailed blank drive or return shipping — postage may add cost.
wizard.vmLegalToggle: Scope, licensing, and data handling — read before ordering
wizard.vmLegalP1: The service is meant to produce one or more usable disk images or virtual-disk files (e.g. OVA, QCOW2, or another agreed format) from a typical Windows install before we install Linux. A complete bit-identical clone of every hidden OEM partition, BitLocker state, or firmware variation is not guaranteed.
wizard.vmLegalP2: Running a commercial OS (e.g. Windows) inside a VM or moved to other hardware is governed by software licence terms and vendor rules. Sparkki does not grant licences; the customer is responsible for compliance.
wizard.vmLegalP3: Images may contain personal or sensitive data. We retain working copies only as long as fulfilment requires; you control retention and disposal of the media we hand back.
wizard.vmLegalP4: If the machine will not boot normally or disks are encrypted, image quality or feasibility may be limited — we will tell you before work starts.
wizard.contactHint: Confirmation and pickup instructions go here. No name is needed at this step.
wizard.stripeMissing: Payments disabled: set STRIPE_SECRET_KEY in the environment.
wizard.errorGeneric: Checkout failed. Try again or contact us.
wizard.validationComputerShort: Description is too short — enter at least three characters (model or a short note).
wizard.validationContactEmpty: Enter a phone number or email before payment.
wizard.validationContactInvalid: Check the format: use an email or phone number (e.g. +358…).

b2b.errorSend: Sending failed. Try again shortly or email tuki@sparkki.fi.

b2b.fieldDetailsPlaceholder: e.g. We have 15 old Dell laptops…
b2b.fieldDetailsHint: Model names, device counts, timing — whatever is on your mind.
b2b.fieldContactHint: We reply using the channel you provide.

tuki.title: Support
tuki.intro: We help before and after installation.
tuki.bookingIntro: Pick a slot in the Calendly scheduler below (shown when the public scheduling link is configured).
tuki.bookingNotConfigured: Scheduling is not configured yet. Add NEXT_PUBLIC_CALENDLY_EMBED_URL to the project `.env` or your host environment (see docs/calendly-booking.md).
tuki.bookingInvalidUrl: NEXT_PUBLIC_CALENDLY_EMBED_URL is not valid. Use an HTTPS link like https://calendly.com/you/event-name (Calendly → Event types → Copy link).
tuki.formIntro: Pick a channel. Writing a message? Two fields is enough.
tuki.discordHelp: Join the server — quick answers from the community and us.
tuki.callHelp: Weekdays 10:00–17:00. Best when you need an immediate conversation.
tuki.formWhatPlaceholder: What happened? You can write in Finnish or English.
tuki.formContactHint: We can look up your order from this when needed.
tuki.formSent: Thanks! Your message was sent. We will get back to you as soon as we can.
tuki.formError: Send failed. Try again in a moment or email us directly.
tuki.formNotConfigured: Form not enabled: set SUPPORT_NOTIFY_EMAIL and RESEND_API_KEY.
tuki.formRateLimit: Too many submissions. Wait a moment and try again.
tuki.formValidationMessageShort: Please write a bit more (at least 4 characters).
tuki.formValidationContactEmpty: Enter an email or phone number so we can reply.
tuki.formValidationContactInvalid: Use a valid email or phone number (e.g. +358…).

yhteiso.title: Community
yhteiso.intro: Join Discord — questions, tips, and peer help.
yhteiso.discordBlockLead: Ask about installs, hardware, and distros — we and the community help out.
yhteiso.youtubeIntro: Short how-tos and demos on our YouTube channel.
yhteiso.guidelinesBody: Be kind, no spam. Focus on computers and Linux.
yhteiso.widgetIntro: See who is online and join the server (when the server ID is configured).

admin.signInFailed: Sign in failed. Check email and password.
admin.aiTestingIntro: Exercise the public POST /api/public/laptop-specs endpoint the same way the order flow and order detail do. Responses use SearXNG and an optional LLM (SPECS_* env).
admin.checksIntro: Latest anonymous runs from the public compatibility API. Used for aggregate reporting (Feature 4 groundwork).
admin.checksEmptyDescription: When customers use the order wizard or other flows that call POST /api/compatibility, rows appear here automatically.
admin.ordersStub: Manage orders and send completion emails.
admin.ordersEmptyDescription: New service orders appear here after customers complete checkout.
admin.ordersEmptyFilteredDescription: Try a different search term or status filter.
admin.usbOrdersIntro: Linux USB stick orders (pending and paid).
admin.usbOrdersEmptyDescription: Linux USB stick orders show here once customers place them.
admin.careSubscriptionsEmptyDescription: Stripe Care Basic subscriptions will list here when active.
admin.portableVmOpsChecklist:
1) Confirm customer handoff choice (USB/NAS vs. shipped media) and sufficient capacity.
2) Log agreed format (OVA, QCOW2, raw, …) and verification (boot test / hash).
3) Check BitLocker/encryption and BIOS constraints before signing off the capture.
4) Hand media only to the named customer or agreed courier — no long-term storage without contract.
5) Document gaps (missing partition, unreadable disk) in internal notes.
admin.modelsIntro: Add models and complete checks. Unchecked items are listed first.
admin.modelsErrorDuplicate: This make and model already exists.
admin.modelCompatibleHint: Saving marks the model approved or rejected and stamps the review.
admin.guidesIntro: Edit MDX content and publishing. Metadata is stored in the database.
admin.guidesNewIntro: Write MDX body only; front matter is regenerated on save.
admin.guideErrSlug: Slug may only contain lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens.
admin.guideErrMinutes: Duration must be at least 1 minute.
admin.guideSaveHint: Save writes content/guides/ and updates the database.
